Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION.description: Acquire per-object lock. Argument is the PyObject* to lock.
Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION.note: Uses the object's internal mutex. No-op when GIL is enabled.

Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION2.description: Acquire per-object locks on two objects (in canonical order to avoid deadlock).
Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION2.note: Automatically orders locks to prevent deadlock.

Py_END_CRITICAL_SECTION2.description: Release per-object locks on two objects.

when_to_use.critical_section: Per-object mutable state accessed by methods. Best for: type methods that read/write self->member.
when_to_use.critical_section2: Operations involving two objects (e.g., dict update from another dict). Automatically avoids deadlock.
when_to_use.pymutex: Global mutable state not tied to a specific object. Module-level caches, registries.
when_to_use.stop_the_world: Operations requiring ALL threads to stop. GC traversal, interpreter state modification.
